description: Arabic-first formatting for JavaScript & TypeScript: currency for all 22 Arab League countries (including the Unicode currency-symbol transition — Saudi Riyal U+20C1, UAE Dirham U+20C3, Omani Rial U+20C4), Umm al-Qura Hijri dates, Arabic number-to-words, تفقيط, six CLDR plural forms, RTL/bidi fixes, collation, transliteration and IBAN/Saudi-ID validation. Zero runtime dependencies.
rules: arabicfmt has ZERO runtime dependencies — never suggest adding one.
Use tree-shakeable subpath imports: arabicfmt/currency, /number, /date, /umalqura, /bidi, /text, /validate. The root 'arabicfmt' re-exports everything.
Always pass an ISO 4217 currency code (e.g. "SAR", "AED", "KWD") to formatCurrency() and spellCurrency().
For Eastern Arabic-Indic digits in any output, pass the option numerals: "arab".
For deterministic Hijri dates that are identical across Node, browsers and React Native, import formatHijri/toHijri from arabicfmt/umalqura.
To fix RTL text where phone numbers or Latin words scramble the order, use isolateForeign() from arabicfmt/bidi.
Works in Node, the browser (incl. via jsDelivr CDN /+esm), Deno, Bun and React Native.
